episode_title: AI is getting more useful — and the risk controls matter more
selected_story_title: OpenAI’s faster model still needs a review layer
selection_reason: This is the clearest lead story for the edition because it captures the main theme: newer AI tools are becoming more capable, but the practical question for small teams is still control, review, and safe use. It also gives the most actionable guidance for creators and businesses trying AI in real workflows.
first_spoken_line: If you have been waiting for a moment when AI starts to feel less like a demo and more like a useful work tool, this week brings another step in that direction. But the bigger story is not just that the tools are getting better. It is that the review layer around them matters even more.
final_spoken_line: If you want the shortest version of today’s brief, it is this: the tools are improving, but the control layer is now part of the job. Start with low-risk drafting and capture. Verify before you send. And build your workflow as if the model is helpful, not infallible.
validation_warnings: Script is shorter than target: 1144 words.
